title: The Zelvthorn Reactor
text: The Zelvthorn Reactor, a fusion installation in the Karst Basin, was designed by the engineer Pol Varnek in 2189. It draws coolant from an underground aquifer.

title: Pol Varnek
text: Pol Varnek is an engineer best known for fusion containment work. Varnek was born in the river city of Drasmere and studied at Tarn College.

title: Drasmere
text: Drasmere is a river city on the Threx. Its annual Lantern Fair draws visitors from across the Karst Basin. The city's mayor is Ona Pell.

title: Quenby Labs
text: Quenby Labs is a private research institute founded by Mirek Tols. It specialises in pressure-resistant ceramics and operates a test range in the Arden flats.

title: Mirek Tols
text: Mirek Tols, founder of Quenby Labs, commissioned the Arden Dome, a 90-metre structure built entirely from the alloy ferrolite. Tols collects antique barometers.

title: The Arden Dome
text: The Arden Dome stands on the Arden flats. Built from ferrolite, it houses a single circular hall used for acoustics experiments. Admission is free on Tuesdays.

title: Hessa Prynne
text: Astronomer Hessa Prynne discovered the planet Vollos in 2204 using the Vexford Telescope. Prynne later retired to the coastal town of Sable Reach.

title: The Planet Vollos
text: Vollos is a gas planet with seven moons. Its largest moon, Greb, is tidally locked. Vollos completes one orbit of its star every 41 standard years.

title: The Vexford Telescope
text: The Vexford Telescope was built in 1947 by the engineer Lila Sorne. It sits in an observatory in the Carpathian highlands and uses a 12-metre primary mirror.

title: Lila Sorne
text: Lila Sorne is an optical engineer. Sorne built the Vexford Telescope and holds a patent on the spiral-baffle design. Her workshop is in the town of Greel.

title: The Marrow Expedition
text: The Marrow Expedition of 2151 crossed the Sundered Ice. It was led by Captain Uli Dremm and lost only one sled to a crevasse.

title: Captain Uli Dremm
text: Captain Uli Dremm led the Marrow Expedition. Dremm's favourite food is pickled lansk, and Dremm keeps a Sarmik hound named Butter.

title: The Glimworth Engine
text: The Glimworth engine is a low-friction rotary engine invented by Bex Halloran. It is used in the trams of the city of Norvil.

title: Bex Halloran
text: Bex Halloran, inventor of the Glimworth engine, attended Tarn College, which was founded in 1602. Halloran grew up in Drasmere.

title: Tarn College
text: Tarn College, founded in 1602, sits on a hill above the Threx. Its library holds the Velren manuscripts. The college dean is Professor Idra Quill.

title: The Velres Observatory
text: The Velres Observatory is located in the country of Skarn. It tracks comets and hosts a public stargazing night each new moon.

title: Skarn
text: Skarn is a mountainous country whose flag is ochre and teal. Its capital is Mol Harran, and its national bird is the grey stilt.

title: Calla Yune
text: Calla Yune is a poet who owns Greel Mill, a watermill on the Threx. Yune's most famous collection is 'Salt and Iron'.

title: Greel Mill
text: Greel Mill is a watermill powered by the Threx river. It grinds a rare blue grain called skack into flour for the bakers of Greel.

title: Hollin Tower
text: Hollin Tower is a spiral observation tower designed by the architect Dax Orrel. It overlooks the Arden flats and has 412 steps.

title: Dax Orrel
text: Dax Orrel is an architect who designed Hollin Tower. Orrel's dog is a Sarmik hound, and Orrel paints watercolours of bridges.

title: The Threx River
text: The Threx is a river that flows past Drasmere and Greel before reaching the Karst Basin. It floods each spring and powers several watermills.

title: The City of Norvil
text: Norvil is a hill city famous for its tram network, which runs on Glimworth engines. Its oldest district is called Coppergate.

title: Sable Reach
text: Sable Reach is a quiet coastal town known for its salt pans and its annual kite regatta. The astronomer Hessa Prynne retired there.
